# Zig Backend Reference

Generated by `src/backend/zig.zig` and `src/backend/zig_typeobject.zig`.

---

## Output Files

**Default mode:** One `<stem>.zig` file containing all types.

**Split-files mode (`--split-files`):** One `.zig` file per top-level IDL module
(e.g. `DDS.zig`, `Foo.zig`), plus a root `<stem>.zig` that re-exports each.
Items not inside any top-level module are emitted inline in the root file.

**File header (all modes):**
```zig
// Generated by zidl from <stem>.idl — DO NOT EDIT

const std = @import("std");
const zidl_rt = @import("zidl_rt");   // omitted with --no-typesupport
```

---

## Type Mapping

| IDL type | Zig type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `boolean` | `bool` | |
| `octet` / `uint8` | `u8` | |
| `int8` | `i8` | |
| `char` | `u8` | |
| `wchar` | `u16` | |
| `short` / `int16` | `i16` | |
| `unsigned short` / `uint16` | `u16` | |
| `long` / `int32` | `i32` | |
| `unsigned long` / `uint32` | `u32` | |
| `long long` / `int64` | `i64` | |
| `unsigned long long` / `uint64` | `u64` | |
| `float` | `f32` | |
| `double` | `f64` | |
| `long double` | `f128` | |
| `string` | `[]const u8` | unbounded |
| `string<N>` | `zidl_rt.BoundedArray(u8, N)` | |
| `wstring` | `[]const u16` | unbounded |
| `wstring<N>` | `zidl_rt.BoundedArray(u16, N)` | |
| `sequence<T>` | `std.ArrayListUnmanaged(T)` | unbounded |
| `sequence<T, N>` | `zidl_rt.BoundedArray(T, N)` | |
| `T[N1][N2]` | `[N1][N2]T` | row-major |
| `@optional T` | `?T` | default = `null` |
| `any` / `Object` / `ValueBase` | `*anyopaque` | |
| `fixed<D,S>` | `f64` | approximate |
| `map<K,V>` | `std.ArrayHashMapUnmanaged(K, V, …)` | |
| Named struct | `T` (direct, by value) | |
| Named enum | `T` (non-exhaustive Zig enum) | |

---

## Generated Type Shapes

### Module
IDL modules become nested namespace structs:
```zig
pub const DDS = struct {
    // ... nested items ...
}; // DDS
```

### Struct
```zig
pub const MyStruct = struct {
    // If there is a base type (struct inheritance):
    _base: BaseType = .{},
    // Members with defaults:
    x: i32 = 0,
    name: []const u8 = "",
    xs: std.ArrayListUnmanaged(i32) = .{},
    // @optional members:
    tag: ?i32 = null,

    // CDR serialization (unless --no-typesupport):
    pub fn serialize(writer: anytype, value: *const MyStruct) !void { ... }
    pub fn deserialize(reader: anytype, alloc: std.mem.Allocator) !MyStruct { ... }
    // Only if at least one @key member:
    pub fn serializeKey(writer: anytype, value: *const MyStruct) !void { ... }
    pub fn deserializeKey(reader: *zidl_rt.CdrReader, alloc: std.mem.Allocator) !MyStruct { ... }
    pub fn deserializeKeyInto(out: *MyStruct, reader: *zidl_rt.CdrReader, alloc: std.mem.Allocator) !void { ... }
    pub fn computeKeyHash(value: MyStruct) [16]u8 { ... }

    // TypeObject/TypeIdentifier (unless --no-typeobject-support):
    pub const type_object: []const u8 = &[_]u8{ 0x00, 0x07, ... };
    pub const equivalence_hash: [14]u8 = [14]u8{ ... };
    pub const type_identifier: [32]u8 = [32]u8{ ... };
}; // MyStruct
```

### Union
IDL unions map to a struct with a discriminant field `_d` and a Zig anonymous union `_u`:
```zig
pub const MyUnion = struct {
    _d: i32 = 0,
    _u: union {
        x: i32,
        name: []const u8,
    } = undefined,

    pub fn serialize(writer: anytype, value: @This()) !void { ... }
    pub fn deserializeInto(reader: anytype, alloc: std.mem.Allocator) !MyUnion { ... }
}; // MyUnion
```

### Enum
Non-exhaustive Zig enum (allows unknown wire values for DDS evolution):
```zig
pub const Color = enum(u32) {
    RED = 0,
    GREEN = 1,
    BLUE = 2,
    _,
}; // Color
```
Storage type is `u32` by default; `@bit_bound(N)` maps to `u8`, `u16`, or `u32`.

### Bitmask
```zig
pub const MyBitmask = u32;
pub const MyBitmask_FLAG_A: MyBitmask = 1 << 0;
pub const MyBitmask_FLAG_B: MyBitmask = 1 << 1;
```

### Bitset
```zig
pub const MyBitset = packed struct {
    field_a: u3 = 0,
    field_b: u5 = 0,
    // unnamed gaps: comment only
    // 8 bits padding (unnamed)
}; // MyBitset
```

### Typedef
```zig
pub const MyTypedef = i32;
pub const Matrix = [4][4]f32;  // array typedef
```

### Native
```zig
pub const MyNative = opaque{}; // @native
```

### Exception
```zig
// IDL exception
pub const MyException = struct {
    code: i32 = 0,
    message: []const u8 = "",
}; // MyException
```

### Interface (without `--generate-interfaces`)
```zig
// IDL interface MyInterface — vtable struct emitted with --generate-interfaces
```

### Interface (with `--generate-interfaces`)
Emits a Zig fat-pointer vtable struct:
```zig
pub const MyInterface = struct {
    ptr: *anyopaque,
    vtable: *const Vtable,

    pub const Vtable = struct {
        deinit: *const fn (ptr: *anyopaque) void,
        // One function pointer per operation:
        my_op: *const fn (ptr: *anyopaque, param: i32) i32,
        // One pair per non-readonly attribute:
        get_my_attr: *const fn (ptr: *anyopaque) i32,
        set_my_attr: *const fn (ptr: *anyopaque, value: i32) void,
    };

    // Forwarding methods:
    pub fn my_op(self: @This(), param: i32) i32 {
        return self.vtable.my_op(self.ptr, param);
    }
    pub fn get_my_attr(self: @This()) i32 { ... }
    pub fn set_my_attr(self: @This(), value: i32) void { ... }
    pub fn deinit(self: @This()) void { ... }
}; // MyInterface
```

Multiple inheritance is handled by flattening — all base interface operations
and attributes are collected (base first) and included in the derived vtable.

### Const
```zig
pub const MAX_SIZE: i32 = 100;
pub const PI: f64 = 3.14159;
pub const LABEL: []const u8 = "hello";
```

---

## CDR Serialization API

Generated `serialize` and `deserialize` methods use `anytype` duck-typing
so they work with any `CdrWriter`/`CdrReader` variant (XCDR1 or XCDR2).

### Writing

```zig
var buf = std.ArrayListUnmanaged(u8).empty;
defer buf.deinit(alloc);
var w = zidl_rt.CdrWriter(.xcdr2).init(&buf, alloc);
try w.writeEncapHeader();
try MyStruct.serialize(&w, &my_value);
// buf.items is the complete CDR stream
```

### Reading

```zig
var r = try zidl_rt.CdrReader.init(cdr_bytes);
var value = try MyStruct.deserialize(&r, alloc);
defer value.deinit(alloc);  // frees heap fields (strings, sequences)
```

### Key Serialization and Hashing

For topic types with `@key` members:

```zig
var buf = std.ArrayListUnmanaged(u8).empty;
defer buf.deinit(alloc);
var w = zidl_rt.CdrWriter(.xcdr2).init(&buf, alloc);
try w.writeEncapHeader();
try MyStruct.serializeKey(&w, &my_value);
```

Generated keyed structs also provide:

```zig
var r = try zidl_rt.CdrReader.init(full_sample_cdr);
const key_only = try MyStruct.deserializeKey(&r, alloc);
const key_hash: [16]u8 = MyStruct.computeKeyHash(key_only);
```

`deserializeKey` reads key fields from a full serialized sample and skips non-key
fields. `computeKeyHash` serializes only key fields as canonical PLAIN_CDR2
big-endian and returns the RTPS key hash: zero-padded serialized key when the
serialized key is at most 16 bytes, otherwise MD5.

### @appendable and DHEADER

When `TypeAnnotations.extensibility == .appendable`:
```zig
// Generated serialize includes DHEADER framing:
const dheader_off = try writer.reserveDheader();
// ... serialize fields ...
writer.patchDheader(dheader_off);
```

On the reader side, `deserialize` calls `try reader.skipDheaderIfXcdr2()` at entry.

### @optional members

Optional (`?T`) members are serialized with a 1-byte presence flag:
```zig
// serialize
try writer.writeBool(value.field != null);
if (value.field) |f| try serializeT(writer, f);

// deserialize
const present = try reader.readBool();
value.field = if (present) try deserializeT(reader, alloc) else null;
```

---

## TypeObject Constants

Every struct body (unless `--no-typeobject-support`) includes:

```zig
/// Full XCDR2 LE MinimalTypeObject bytes (encap header + payload).
pub const type_object: []const u8 = &[_]u8{ 0x00, 0x07, 0x00, 0x00, … };

/// EquivalenceHash = MD5(type_object)[0..14]
/// The on-wire DDS type match identifier used by Cyclone DDS, FastDDS, etc.
pub const equivalence_hash: [14]u8 = [14]u8{ … };

/// SHA-256 fingerprint — zidl convention only, not transmitted.
pub const type_identifier: [32]u8 = [32]u8{ … };
```

See `docs/xtypes_typeobject.md` for the full encoding format.

---

## `@verbatim` Injection

The Zig backend checks raw annotations on each type declaration for `@verbatim`
with `language == "zig"` or `language == "*"`, and injects the `text` parameter
at the appropriate `placement` position:

| `placement` | Position in output |
|---|---|
| `BEGIN_FILE` | Before first declaration |
| `BEFORE_DECLARATION` | Immediately before this type |
| `BEGIN_DECLARATION` | Inside the type body, before members |
| `END_DECLARATION` | Inside the type body, after members |
| `AFTER_DECLARATION` | Immediately after this type |
| `END_FILE` | After last declaration |

---

## `--type-prefix`

When `--type-prefix <pfx>` is set, all generated top-level type names are
prefixed: `pub const <pfx>MyStruct = struct { … }`.

Qualified references within types use the prefixed form for the leaf segment:
e.g. `DDS::Duration_t` → `DDS.<pfx>Duration_t`.

---

## Memory and Allocation

- Primitive scalar fields have zero-value defaults; no allocation.
- `[]const u8` (string) fields default to `""` (empty slice, no allocation).
- `std.ArrayListUnmanaged(T)` (sequence) fields default to `.{}` (empty, no allocation).
- `zidl_rt.BoundedArray(T, N)` fields default to zero-length.
- On `deserialize`, heap-allocated fields (strings, sequences) are allocated
  with the provided `alloc`; caller is responsible for `deinit`.
- Generated `deinit(alloc)` method frees all heap fields recursively (where
  the allocator is needed). For types with no heap fields, no `deinit` is generated.
